source_agent.source_text: Psalm 18 source text: I love you, O LORD, my strength.

translator.working_rendering: I love You, LORD, my strength.

chapter_design_mapper.chapter_start: I love You, LORD, my strength. Rescued love.
chapter_design_mapper.chapter_end: Praise among the nations; steadfast love to David and his seed forever.
chapter_design_mapper.divine_action: The LORD hears, bows the heavens, comes down, and draws His anointed from many waters.
chapter_design_mapper.central_theological_claim: The LORD does not merely send rescue. He comes as rescue.
chapter_design_mapper.christward_fulfillment: The greater Anointed One enters death, is raised, and brings praise among the nations.
chapter_design_mapper.reader_felt_experience: A rescued worshiper moves from fear to praise.
chapter_design_mapper.chapter_design_summary: Rescued love becomes royal and worldwide praise.

canonist.pathway: David's anointed seed and praise among nations.

risks.why_it_matters: Fulfillment must arise from chapter logic.

director.threshold_phrase_rationale: David's love names the relationship before the cosmic rescue unfolds.

composer.title: Love Before Thunder
composer.introduction:
Love speaks before thunder.

David does not begin Psalm 18 with the shaking earth, the smoking heavens, or the arrows of God. He begins with a sentence as intimate as breath: “I love You, LORD, my strength.” The storm comes later. First comes the love of a rescued man.
composer.reflection: The LORD hears from His temple, bows the heavens, and draws David from deep waters. Rescue is not an abstract gift sent from afar; the living God comes near and sets His servant in a broad place.
composer.christ_fulfillment: Jesus is the greater Anointed One who enters the deep waters of death, is raised by the Father, and carries the song of God's steadfast love among the nations.
composer.application: Name the distress honestly, then call upon the LORD. Receive His nearness as the heart of rescue, and let gratitude widen into praise before others.
composer.prayer: Father, hear our cry. Be our rock and fortress. Draw us from deep waters. Set our feet in a broad place. Make our rescued love become praise among the nations. Through Jesus Christ our Lord. Amen.
composer.next_in_sequence: Psalm 19 — The heavens declare the glory of God.

poet.poem:
The Lord has heard my cry today
He drew me from deep sea
He bowed the heavens down to save
And set his love on me

The cords of death wrapped my feet
He heard my cry at sea
He drew me out from waters deep
And set his love on me

The Lord became my living rock
My shield in fear and sea
He brought me into spacious grace
And set his love on me

Now praise will rise among the lands
His mercy runs to me
His king now sings the faithful name
And set his love on me
